title: Zeltnera beyrichii
taxonomy: Zeltnera beyrichii is classified under the plant kingdom, specifically belonging to the family Gentianaceae. This family is commonly known as the gentian or gentianwort family. Zeltnera beyrichii further belongs to the genus Zeltnera, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Within the genus Zeltnera, Zeltnera beyrichii is identified as a species of gentian. It is native to Mexico.

intro: Zeltnera beyrichii, also known as Penstemon beyrichii, is a perennial plant species in the Penstemon genus, native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Zeltnera beyrichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, colds, and coughs.
